natural_language_statement: Consider the composite problem min_x f(x) + h(x), where f is convex with L-Lipschitz gradient and h is closed convex (continuous on univ). The proximal gradient method x_{k+1} = prox_{t · h}(x_k - t · f'(x_k)) with step t ≤ 1/L produces, starting from x_0 with minimizer xm, the bound (f + h)(x_k) - (f + h)(xm) ≤ ‖x_0 - xm‖^2 / (2 · k · t) for all k ≥ 1.
assumptions: E is a complete real inner product space (ProperSpace E)
f, h : E → ℝ both convex on univ
f' is the gradient of f and is L-Lipschitz (L > 0)
x_{k+1} = prox_{t · h}(x_k - t · f'(x_k)); x_0 fixed
conclusion: ∀ (k : ℕ+), (f (alg.x k) + h (alg.x k) - f alg.xm - h alg.xm) ≤ 1 / (2 * k * alg.t) * ‖x₀ - alg.xm‖ ^ 2
proof_sketch: Define gradient mapping G_t(x) = (1/t) (x - prox_{t·h}(x - t · f'(x))). Then x - t·G_t(x) = next iterate when applied at the iterate.
By prox subdifferential characterization, G_t(x) - f'(x) ∈ ∂h(x - t·G_t(x)).
Descent lemma on f: f(x - t·G_t(x)) ≤ f(x) - t·⟨f'(x), G_t(x)⟩ + t^2·L/2·‖G_t(x)‖^2 ≤ f(x) - t·⟨f'(x), G_t(x)⟩ + t/2·‖G_t(x)‖^2 since t·L ≤ 1.
Subgradient inequality on h: h(x - t·G_t(x)) ≤ h(z) - ⟨G_t(x) - f'(x), z - x + t·G_t(x)⟩.
First-order convex inequality on f: f(x) + ⟨f'(x), z - x⟩ ≤ f(z).
Combine to get: φ(x - t·G_t(x)) ≤ φ(z) + ⟨G_t(x), x - z⟩ - (t/2)·‖G_t(x)‖^2 where φ = f + h.
Specialize z = xm and rewrite ⟨G_t(x), x - xm⟩ - (t/2)‖G_t‖^2 = (1/(2t))(‖x - xm‖^2 - ‖x - t·G_t(x) - xm‖^2).
Conclude per-step bound; sum / multiply by k to get O(1/T).
